summary: Standard ultralocal cluster-expansion theorem with exact smallness threshold, local influence and the 3*kappa0+3 decay loss.
Lemma 28 resolves C_{k,r} into local algebraic factors and a Green operator, the identity later localized by random-walk expansions.
Exact small-field minimizer expansion, fluctuation covariance and one-step Gaussian normalization/scaling formulas.
Uniform summability of connected M-polymers pinned at one block, by size and by tree distance.
Random-walk expansion of G_k and uniform exponential block-distance estimates for G_k and differentiated variants.
Polymer counting and metric-weight summability around a pinned cube or an intersected target polymer.
Bibliographic spine locating the main Balaban lattice-gauge papers and Cammarota CMP85 from one checked primary reference list.
Lemma 21 turns the localized small-field fluctuation integral into an exponential of local activities with explicit scale and metric decay.
Exact proof-stage chain from Ω-local geometry through metric stitching to the first activity K(Y) and its ultralocal integral K#(Y).
Second Ursell exponentiation defining H# from K#, with the Ω-connected Ursell coefficient and exact union constraint.
Lemma 3.18 applies Appendix F to the localized large-field fluctuation integral, with explicit t/u radii, source input bound and final metric loss.
Section 3.8 defines the restricted fluctuation covariance, the nonlocal square-root change of variables and the resolvent/random-walk localization interface.
Removal and localization of boundary-dependent terms using local influence and a Cauchy contour representation.
Uniform pinned summability for polymers with holes in the modified metric d_M(-, mod Omega^c).
Theorem 3.1 states the multiscale large/small-field representation and repeats the exact global normalization recursion under explicit large-L, large-M and small-coupling conditions.
Final cluster activity estimate and stability corollary, uniform in the ultraviolet cutoff and volume index.
Theorem 1 collects the convergent scalar RG representation and the exact local E, R and active-boundary B decay estimates.
